ELR Name: SOUTH WALES MAIN LINE (GLOUCESTER YD JN - NEYLAND)
Free Text: Hazel dormouse have been confirmed present at this location following survey. This area is subject to a capital delivery and maintenance European protected species licences until 31/12/2025 and 31/12/2023 (both licences are in the process of being renewed) for the permanent and temporary clearance of vegetation to ensure the safe running of the railway and to facilitate the land slip works. This will include felling of dead, diseased and dangerous trees to prevent train derailment, cutting vegetation to ground level for a safe walking route, and work to maintain vegetation at these levels. No other planned works can happen within this area other than that stipulated within the license works schedules and method statements (location of each work type), and subject to an amendment. Monitoring boxes have been installed along the boundary and are not to be disturbed.  Ecovigour (07538275034) (Capital Delivery licence) and Thomson (Maintenance licence) are the Named Ecologists.

WE RAN A TIME TRIAL USING TRAFFIC IN THE UP DIRECTIION AT 95MPH USING 1 X SITE 1 X INTER AND 1 X DISTANT LOOKOUT AND OVER THE 3 OCASSIONS WE DID SO WE WERE IN A POSITION OF SAFETY FOR 30.4 SECONDS 26.6 SEC AND 25.2 SEC FROM WHEN SITE LOOKOUT WARNED THE GROUP AND ACKNOWLEDGED THE INTERMEDIATE LOOKOUT. DEAN LLOYD <ACTING TSM NEWPORT> STEVE BURLEY <H+S REP> WERE IN ATTENDANCE AND BOTH AGREE THAT THE RESTRICTION COULD BE CONSIDERED FOR REMOVAL FOR INSPECTION PURPOSES".
